concepts.title: Indentation in Python
concepts.description: Indentation is crucial in Python as it defines the block of code. Four spaces or a tab are used for indentation. Incorrect indentation can lead to syntax errors.

concepts.title: String Methods
concepts.description: String methods in Python are frequently asked in exams. Some popular methods include upper(), lower(), split(), join(), find(), and replace().

concepts.title: List Comprehension
concepts.description: List comprehension is a powerful feature in Python that simplifies the process of creating lists from existing lists or other iterables. It is represented by a syntax like [expression for variable in iterable].

concepts.title: Use of Single Quotes and Double Quotes
concepts.description: Students often use single quotes for string literals and double quotes for variable names. However, in Python, single quotes are used for string literals and double quotes for variable names. This can lead to errors if not done correctly.

concepts.title: Order of Operators in Python
concepts.description: The order of operators in Python is PEMDAS (Parentheses, Exponents, Multiplication and Division, Addition and Subtraction). This helps in remembering the order of operations.

concepts.title: Importing Modules in Python
concepts.description: When importing modules in Python, the 'from' keyword is used to import specific functions or variables from a module, while the 'import' keyword is used to import the entire module.

concepts.title: Exception Handling
concepts.description: Exception handling is a crucial feature in Python that helps in handling runtime errors. It is represented by a 'try-except' block, where 'try' block contains the code that might raise an exception and 'except' block contains the code that handles the exception.

concepts.title: Lambda Functions
concepts.description: Lambda functions in Python are small anonymous functions that can take any number of arguments but can have only one expression. They are used for operations that require a small function and then discarded.
